attempts.description: After clicking '+ New conversation' in the Canvas palette, the agent was created server-side and added to the Conversations list on the left, but no node was placed on the canvas itself — operator stared at empty grid wondering where the new agent went. Fixed: PalettePanel emits a new `conversation_created` Qt signal; CanvasPage handles it by dropping a ConversationNode at the centre of the current viewport AND auto-opening the chat dialog so the operator immediately sees and can talk to the agent. Idempotent — duplicate creates re-centre + reopen the existing node. Also replaced 'Unnamed conversation' fallback with a friendlier `<model> · <HH:MM>` default name so multiple un-named agents are distinguishable.
change_overview: apps/gui/canvas/palette.py — new `conversation_created` Signal; `_do_create` emits the enriched agent dict after agents.create (and the optional first-send) succeeds
apps/gui/canvas/palette.py — friendlier default name when operator leaves Name field blank (`<model> · <HH:MM>`)
apps/gui/canvas/page.py — `_on_conversation_created` drops a ConversationNode at view-centre, refreshes lineage, and opens the chat dialog
attempts.risk_note: Pure additive: new signal + handler. No existing palette / canvas paths changed. The view-centre placement uses `view.mapToScene` which is correct regardless of zoom / pan; tested mentally against 100% zoom and the empty-canvas state from the screenshot.
next_steps: Run scripts/restart.cmd so the GUI picks up the new code
Click Canvas → Conversations '+ New' → Create — the new agent should appear at the centre of the canvas with the chat dialog already open
attempts.annotation_excerpt: I don't get it, i've created a new agent

attempts.description: The 'Probe failed: unknown method: limits.check' error is the JSON-RPC server's generic response when an RPC method isn't in its in-memory registration table. The `limits.check` handler IS registered in `apps/service/main.py` (verified line 1855), so the only way the running service can return 'unknown method' is if it's an OLDER service process from before that registration landed in main. The user merged the relevant PR + ran update.cmd (which `git pull`s + `pip install -e`s), but the orchestrator service was already running and stayed on the old in-memory code. Operational fix only: restart the service.
next_steps: Run scripts/restart.cmd — the three-pass kill (window-title + port-listening on 8765) reaps any orphaned service, then launches the GUI which auto-spawns a fresh service
Re-open the Limits tab — the cards should populate
